setup: Three rectangles presented via §2.11 Multiple Choice on a single screen: a 3×4 rectangle (area = 12, a multiple of 3); a 2×5 rectangle (area = 10, not a multiple of 3); a 1×7 rectangle (area = 7, not a multiple of 3). Build affordances disabled across all three. Tier: BASELINE.
student_action: Student reads the prompt — "Here are three rectangles. One of them has an area that is a multiple of 3. Tap that rectangle, then tap Check." — taps the chosen rectangle, then taps Check.
teacher_move: Light prompt only. Post-Check correct response: "Your rectangle has 3 rows of 4 squares. The area is 12. That is a multiple of 3." Post-Check incorrect (Light remediation): "Look at the number of rows in each rectangle. Which one has rows of 3 squares?" No re-teaching.
key_observation: Student uses rectangle structure (rows × side length) to recognize a valid multiple relationship without building. Mastery component: CONCEPTUAL (recognition).
common_detour: Student picks based on overall visual size ("looks bigger") rather than row structure.
intervention: Brief redirect to row structure: "Look at the rows. Which rectangle shows rows of 3 squares?" Do not reveal the correct option.
